summary: Prevent template bootstrap from detaching sibling development clones
description: Route the template-managed stack checkout to a dedicated cache path and regression-test that a sibling go-workflow-stack development clone remains on its branch and commit.
acceptance: R-1: Default template bootstrap never fetches into or detaches a sibling go-workflow-stack development checkout.
R-2: The managed immutable runtime lives in a dedicated user cache keyed by the project stack_ref while explicit GO_STACK behavior remains fail-closed.
R-3: A regression proves a sibling development clone keeps its branch and HEAD while default bootstrap resolves the exact project tag.
R-4: Local template and Linux WSL gates pass with GitHub Actions unused.

evidence.summary: R-1 complete: default bootstrap no longer targets ../go-workflow-stack; it uses an isolated tag-keyed XDG or HOME cache and the actual development checkout remained on main 5aab526 with upstream 0/0 throughout final gates. R-2 complete: explicit GO_STACK still requires exact runtime unless the explicit development override is set; managed cache now rejects origin mismatch against GO_STACK_REMOTE. R-3 complete: regression creates annotated v9.9.9, proves cache path and exact peeled commit, preserves sibling main and HEAD, and rejects a different remote reusing the cache. R-4 complete: projected final-state scripts/check.sh and GO_REQUIRE_LIVE_HERMES=1 scripts/check-linux.sh passed from isolated caches; bash syntax and git diff --check passed; GitHub Actions unused. Fresh-eyes re-review verdict A.
